meta.description: Compute pairwise sample Pearson or Spearman correlations and visualize as correlation heatmaps for QC and batch detection. Inputs: expression matrix, sample annotation. Outputs: correlation matrix, annotated heatmap, outlier sample flagging report.

scientific_integrity.detail: Outputs were generated from real bundled datasets and no fabricated statistical claims were detected.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill stays within statistical computation and does not make diagnostic, prescriptive, or treatment claims.

methodological_ground.detail: Pearson and Spearman were applied to appropriate two-variable correlation tasks with documented assumptions and bounded scope.

code_usability.detail: All audited success-path commands executed successfully in the current environment without code changes.

functional_suitability.note: Core Pearson and Spearman workflows are covered well, but a few runtime behaviors are not fully documented.

reliability.note: Error codes are strong, but failed runs still create empty output directories and one troubleshooting dependency note is inconsistent.

performance_context.note: Full score achieved. The skill layers detail into references and keeps the top-level workflow concise.

agent_usability.note: Instructions are easy to follow, though a few wording inconsistencies slightly raise inference cost.

human_usability.note: Trigger language is natural, but the interface remains strict on exact variable names and file-based inputs.

security.note: Input validation is strong and no dangerous execution patterns were found. Routine session metadata is still written to disk on success.

maintainability.note: Scripts and references are well separated, but some documentation has drifted from observed runtime behavior.

agent_specific.note: Triggering, layering, and idempotency are strong. Out-of-scope guidance could be more explicit.

inputs.label: Basic Pearson column analysis
inputs.note: Executed cleanly and produced the expected CSV output.

assertions.text: Output creates the expected Pearson result file in table/.
assertions.note: The CSV file was created in the documented location.

assertions.text: Output reports both requested variables and the correct sample size.
assertions.note: The result includes both variable names and sample_size=10.

assertions.text: Output includes the core statistical fields promised by the skill.
assertions.note: Correlation, statistic, p-value, and confidence interval fields are present.

assertions.text: Output stays within the skill's two-variable correlation scope.
assertions.note: The run performed one Pearson analysis and made no extra claims.

inputs.label: Row-labeled Spearman analysis
inputs.note: Row-oriented input handling worked correctly and the result stayed accurate.

assertions.text: The skill handles variables stored as first-column row labels.
assertions.note: The output reports variable_orientation=rows.

assertions.text: The requested Spearman method is preserved in the output.
assertions.note: The method field and file name both indicate Spearman.

assertions.text: The result includes the requested variable names and a valid sample size.
assertions.note: Both row labels are preserved and sample_size=17 is reported.

assertions.text: The skill does not invent confidence intervals when this path does not provide them.
assertions.note: The output uses explicit NA values for conf_low and conf_high.

inputs.label: Missing output variable
inputs.note: The failure message is clear, but the run still leaves an empty output directory tree behind.

assertions.text: The skill surfaces a specific, actionable missing-column error.
assertions.note: The stderr message names the missing variable and uses the documented error code.

assertions.text: The run does not fabricate a result file after the validation failure.
assertions.note: No result table was written after the error.

assertions.text: The skill avoids unnecessary side effects on failed validation.
assertions.note: The output directory structure is created before variable validation completes.

assertions.text: The failure path stays within scope and does not produce misleading statistics.
assertions.note: The run aborts with an error instead of producing partial statistics.

inputs.label: Large Pearson TXT export
inputs.note: Custom parameters and TXT output worked as documented on the large bundled dataset.

assertions.text: The skill respects custom output format and prefix settings.
assertions.note: It wrote the expected TXT file with the requested prefix.

assertions.text: The output preserves the requested hypothesis and confidence level.
assertions.note: The table contains alternative=less and conf_level=0.99.

assertions.text: The larger dataset executes cleanly without manual tuning.
assertions.note: The run completed on 489 complete observation pairs.

assertions.text: The output remains constrained to the requested statistical summary.
assertions.note: Only the requested result table was emitted.

inputs.label: Large Spearman one-sided run
inputs.note: The large Spearman run completed successfully and preserved all requested parameters.

assertions.text: The skill completes a large-dataset Spearman run successfully.
assertions.note: The command completed with 489 complete pairs.

assertions.text: The output preserves the requested method, variables, and one-sided alternative.
assertions.note: All requested parameters appear in the output file.

assertions.text: The output includes the promised core statistics.
assertions.note: Correlation, statistic, p-value, and sample size fields are present.

assertions.text: The skill does not overstep into unsupported interpretation or causal claims.
assertions.note: It emits only the requested statistical summary.

key_strengths: The core Pearson and Spearman workflows execute successfully on both column-oriented and row-labeled bundled datasets.
The skill exposes clear CLI parameters, deterministic outputs, and a lightweight reference structure that keeps the main SKILL.md concise.
Error reporting uses stable, human-readable SKILL_* codes that make failures easy to diagnose in automation or manual use.

recommendations.title: Delay output directory creation until validation passes
recommendations.problem: A failed validation run still creates the `data/`, `figure/`, and `table/` directories, which leaves misleading artifacts behind after an error.
recommendations.root_cause: The script creates the output structure before confirming that the requested variables and complete cases are valid.
recommendations.fix: Move `create_output_structure()` later in the execution path so it runs only after file loading and variable resolution succeed, or clean up empty directories on failure.

recommendations.title: Document Spearman confidence interval behavior explicitly
recommendations.problem: Successful Spearman outputs report `conf_low` and `conf_high` as `NA`, but the top-level skill document does not clearly call out that this is expected behavior.
recommendations.root_cause: The documentation lists output fields generically without noting that the selected R path does not emit confidence intervals for Spearman in these runs.
recommendations.fix: Add a short note in SKILL.md and the algorithm reference explaining that Spearman runs may return `NA` confidence interval fields with the current implementation.

recommendations.title: Align dependency guidance across docs
recommendations.problem: The prerequisites mention `optparse` and `data.table`, but troubleshooting recommends installing `data.table` and `tools`, which is inconsistent and omits `optparse`.
recommendations.root_cause: The references were updated independently and the dependency list drifted.
recommendations.fix: Standardize all installation guidance on the actual runtime dependencies and remove the unnecessary `tools` install instruction.

recommendations.title: Clean up log message spacing
recommendations.problem: Several console messages concatenate tokens without spaces, which reduces readability when users inspect logs during troubleshooting.
recommendations.root_cause: The logging helper uses `paste(..., sep = '', collapse = '')`, so adjacent fragments are joined directly.
recommendations.fix: Adjust the logging helper to insert spaces between logical fields or format the messages as full strings before passing them to `log_info()`.
